The source document does not contain any contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, or URLs), and none has been fabricated in the output JSON.

The source document consists of a single page containing a map, a title, a date, a legend, a source attribution, and a scale. All of these components are represented in the output JSON. The map labels are included as a paragraph of text, and the legend is accurately captured as a list.
There are no formal tables in the source document. The legend, which is visually a key, has been appropriately converted into a list format in the JSON, and all 10 entries are present.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the source PDF and the output JSON. The text 'NC Parcels Project Status by County', the date, the legend items, the sources, and the map labels (county abbreviations) appear only once in the source and once in the output. There are no duplicated paragraphs or sections.

The heading hierarchy in the output JSON starts at H2 for the document title and date. It then moves to H3 for 'Parcel Updates:', followed by H4 for the sub-header 'Most recent Quarter'. This represents a logical nested hierarchy. The 'Sources:' section correctly returns to H3. No heading levels are skipped (e.g., H2 to H4).

The source document does not contain any formal tables. The legend section, which is visually organized in the PDF, has been appropriately converted into a list in the output JSON, which is a structurally sound representation for this content.

The reading order in the output JSON flows logically from the top of the document to the bottom: Title -> Date -> Map Image -> Map Labels -> Legend -> Sources -> Scale. This matches the visual and logical intent of the source PDF.

The legend under 'Parcel Updates:' has been correctly converted into an unordered list. Each status and count pair (e.g., '2025 Q2 (83)') is preserved as an individual list item. The nesting from heading to list is correct.
There are no links present in the source document, and none have been fabricated or injected into the output JSON.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document has been fundamentally altered. The source PDF is a single-page map-based report where the map is the central feature, with the legend and logo positioned relative to it. In the HTML, the map graphic is removed and replaced by a dense block of text containing the county names. The logo, which was a small element in the bottom right of the PDF, has been enlarged and moved to a central position. The legend has been converted into a standard vertical list below the text block. This transition from a spatial, graphic-centric layout to a linear, text-centric layout constitutes a major failure in maintaining visual organization.
checks.b_evidence_source: A central map of North Carolina counties with status coloring, with a legend in the bottom-left and a logo in the bottom-right.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML displays the title, followed by a large logo, then a paragraph of county names ('HYDE DARE CART...'), and then a bulleted list for the legend.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; the legend section is a list of categories and counts which is rendered as a list in HTML.

The source PDF contains two meaningful graphics that are missing in the HTML: the colored map of North Carolina counties and the north arrow icon (top left). While the logo has been preserved (albeit resized and split), the map is the primary informative element of the document and its absence as an image is a major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: Colored map of North Carolina counties and a north arrow icon.
checks.b_evidence_output: The map graphic and north arrow are missing; only text labels from the map are present.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of duplicated text blocks or repeated images within the HTML output compared to the source PDF.
